_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about different theories and evidence about early human evolution, migration, and the establishment of the earliest societies, including in Australia.

_source.w: We are learning about different theories and evidence about early human evolution, migration, and the establishment of the earliest societies, including in Australia.
y: Understanding our deep human past connects us to the shared story of all people on Earth.
Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples have the longest continuous cultural history on Earth — this is globally significant.
Examining evidence and interpretations develops our critical thinking about how we know what we know about the past.
eg: Investigating the Out of Africa theory and the evidence supporting human migration across the globe.
Examining archaeological evidence for the early arrival of Aboriginal Peoples in Australia — dating back at least 65,000 years.
Discussing how different types of evidence — fossils, DNA, artefacts — contribute to our understanding of early humans.
Analysing how interpretations of early human history have changed as new evidence has been discovered.
Comparing theories about when and how humans first arrived in Australia.
Discussing the significance of Australia having the world's oldest continuous cultures.
